policy_statement: Ensure all City-funded/directly dependent cultural venues have an action plan aligned with climate objectives (and raise awareness for other venues), including low-carbon transport solutions, Good Food catering, local sourcing (including local currency), and reduced energy use.
mechanism_description: The city undertakes to ensure City-funded cultural venues put in place climate-aligned action plans that reduce energy consumption and promote lower-carbon transport and catering/sourcing, supporting emissions reductions from venue operations and associated activities.
primary_causal_pathway: Institutional planning & operational set → ↓ energy/fuel use + ↓ high-carbon procurement/transport → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change in human activity/system: City governance set publicly funded/controlled entities (e.g., cultural venues, regional/municipal departments) to develop and implement climate-aligned action plans, embedding targets and measures for operations, mobility, and purchasing.  
(2) Mediating physical/process drivers: Implemented measures reduce electricity and thermal demand (efficiency, controls, retrofits), shift travel modes and logistics (modal shift, reduced vehicle-km, electrification/low-carbon transport), and reduce lifecycle emissions from food/catering and sourcing (lower-emission diets, local/low-carbon procurement, waste reduction).  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced direct (Scope 1) and indirect (Scope 2 and selected Scope 3) GHG emissions attributable to facility operations and associated services.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is lowering GHG emissions by requiring climate action plans that drive reduced energy use and lower-carbon transport/procurement in covered institutions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Planning requirements lead to implementation of operational measures that reduce energy/fuel consumption and carbon-intensive activities, yielding net GHG reductions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While energy efficiency and sourcing can be described as “resource efficiency,” the mechanism’s dominant climate-relevant endpoint is emissions reduction (mitigation), and the planning instrument is explicitly climate-aligned (GHG-focused).  
3) Edge cases applied: If a plan were purely procedural (no implementation, no binding measures) or focused mainly on heat/flood preparedness, it could shift toward adaptation; however, the canonical mechanism as stated is an “emissions reduction pathway,” anchoring mitigation as primary.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy that institutionalizes climate action planning explicitly to cut energy use, decarbonize transport, and reduce procurement-related emissions shares the same dominant mitigation outcome regardless of venue/department.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Whether a given policy’s realized reductions come mostly from buildings, transport, food/procurement, or behavior change depends on policy text and implementation evidence and should be assigned at the individual-policy level, not at this mechanism level.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Plans can be non-binding or symbolic; if implementation/enforcement is absent, real-world mitigation impact may be limited even though the intended dominant pathway remains emissions reduction. Some action plans may also include adaptation/resilience elements, but those are secondary unless they dominate the plan’s measures.
key_indicators: "ensure that all cultural venues… funded and directly dependent… have put in place an action plan"; "designed to meet the area's climate objectives"; "proposing transport solutions"; '"Good Food" criteria'; "Promoting local sourcing… local Brussels currency"; "Reduced energy consumption"
co_benefits: Improved local air quality and reduced congestion (via transport solutions); public health benefits (via “Good Food”); local economic development/support for local suppliers (via local sourcing/local currency).
instance_edge_case_notes: The mechanism includes procurement and mobility elements that could also resemble a procurement requirement, but the core instrument is the mandated climate-aligned action plan for venues (a planning/operational requirement) rather than a standalone procurement rule.
